chapter_title.en: Chapter 9 – The gathering of the two Salāts

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/52)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/52)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الصَّلَاتَيْنِ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ بَيْنَهُمَا تَطَوُّعٌ فَإِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا تَطَوُّعٌ فَلَا جَمْعَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Musa, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ibn Fazzal, from Hammad Bin Usman who said, ‘Muhammad Bin Hakeym narrated to me saying,

‘I heard Abu Al-Hassanasws saying: ‘The gathering between the two Salāt is when there does not happen to be Optional Salāts between the two. So when there were Optional Salāts between the two, so do not gather’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn Musa from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Faddal from Hammad ibn ‘Uthman who has said the following: “Muhammad ibn Hakim narrated to me that he had heard abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘Performing two Salah (prayer) soon one after the other is when no optional Salah (prayer) is performed between them. When optional Salah (prayer) is performed between them, they are not performed soon one after the other.’”
